summary.what: Add msg: commit-subject skip rule to .timbersignore and skip release changelog commits via 'msg:chore: changelog for v*'
summary.why: A commit is path-skippable only if every file matches a rule, but release changelog commits also touch the version-badge source file — a subject glob skips them precisely without globally hiding that file's real edits
summary.how: Mirrored the author: glob design: msg: lines parse to a third ignoreLine kind, thread through loadSkipConfig/Storage.skipMessages into classifyByIdentity (reason 'message'); refactored filterByRules to reuse classifyByIdentity, which both flattened the loop under the complexity budget and single-sourced skip semantics with the debug trace; allowlisted .timbersignore in .gitignore so every clone/CI reads the same config

notes: Considered path-based skip first (CHANGELOG.md + site/) but it can't work: filterByRules requires ALL files in a commit to match, and the release commit also touches site/layouts/index.html (version badge) — path-skipping that would hide legit landing-page edits. Message-based is the precise tool and generalizes to other housekeeping subjects (version bumps, release commits). Discovered .timbersignore was gitignored by the repo's allowlist .gitignore — would have silently made the skip clone-local; fixed.
